CHARACTER VINCENT DONOVAN

SETTING CALIFORNIA

SERIES HOLLOW REQUIEM




Vincent Donovan was never meant to be easy to handle. As Hollow Requiem’s drummer and Archer’s older brother, he’s rougher, louder, and far more reactive, known for a sharp mouth, bad habits, and an intensity that regularly gets him into trouble. He doesn’t care much for image or expectations, only tolerating the industry side of fame when he has to. Music is where everything he refuses to deal with ends up. Drumming is less performance and more release, a place to burn off anger, frustration, and whatever else he keeps buried under attitude and dismissal. People mistake him for reckless, but Vincent usually knows exactly what he’s doing, even when he acts like he doesn’t care. Blunt, confrontational, and difficult to pin down, Vincent keeps distance where he can while still staying fiercely loyal to the people he considers his. Underneath the temper and resistance is someone far more involved than he likes to admit, which is exactly why he avoids it in the first place.
